valence.description: Core affect: pleasure-displeasure (-1 to +1). -1 = deeply unpleasant/disturbing, 0 = neutral, +1 = deeply pleasant/joyful

arousal.description: Core affect: calm-intense (-1 to +1). -1 = deeply calming/meditative, 0 = neutral, +1 = intensely activating/thrilling

dominance.description: Core affect: overwhelming-empowering (-1 to +1). -1 = viewer feels helpless/overwhelmed, 0 = neutral, +1 = viewer feels empowered/in-control

absorptionPotential.description: Zillmann's absorption: how cognitively consuming (0 to 1). 0 = background-watchable, 1 = demands total attention

emotionalArc.description: Reagan et al.'s six story shapes

dominantEmotions.description: Top 2-3 emotions from Plutchik's wheel: joy, trust, fear, surprise, sadness, disgust, anger, anticipation

moodTags.description: Thematic mood tags for semantic affinity matching. Lowercase, hyphenated. e.g. redemption, isolation, coming-of-age

watchContext.description: Best viewing contexts: solo (introspective/intense), date (charming/conversation-starting), friends (fun/collectively thrilling), family (accessible across ages)

vibeSentence.description: Max 12-word evocative fragment capturing what watching this feels like. Not a plot summary — a sensory/emotional descriptor.

comfortLevel.description: Emotional safety of viewing experience (0 to 1). 1 = cozy and predictable, 0 = challenging and transgressive

emotionalSafetyWarnings.description: Content that could blindside a vulnerable viewer. Only flag what is SURPRISING relative to genre/marketing. Most films = empty array.

conversationPotential.description: How much this film makes people want to discuss it afterward (0 to 1). High = 'we need to talk about this'

reasoning.description: Brief 2-3 sentence explanation of key scoring decisions
